stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant large language model (LLM).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack targeting Large Language Models (LLMs).
This attack exploits positional encoding within LLMs, moving beyond traditional content-based triggers.
Highlights a significant security vulnerability in LLMs, impacting safety- and privacy-critical applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes key ML technologies such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance on Apple Silicon.
This integration leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for optimization.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are licensed under Apache 2.0.
The partnership involves ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to enhance safety capabilities.

stories.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: YouTube is rolling out its AI likeness detection tool to all adult users (18+).
The system utilizes a selfie-style scan of a user's face for monitoring.
The tool is designed to proactively hunt for and alert users about potential deepfakes of themselves on the platform.

stories.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
stories.author: Kate Knibbs, wired.com
bullet_points: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is actively leveraging AI.
The AI application is focused on detecting insider trading within prediction markets.
This signals a significant regulatory shift toward using advanced technology for financial oversight.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a significant copyright settlement valued at $1.5 billion.
The approval of this historic settlement is facing delays from the judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers attempted to rush the settlement to secure substantial fees.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening in medical imaging.
Addresses limitations in current models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Aims to improve early detection and clinical intervention by providing transparent reasoning pathways.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs with new, domain-specific knowledge.
MeMo achieves this by encoding new knowledge into a dedicated memory model without altering the core LLM parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, making them more suitable for real-world, timely applications.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard unlearning evaluations to account for post-training quantization.
Demonstrates that common forgetting methods lose their effectiveness when language models are compressed (e.g., 4-bit quantization).
Identifies a systematic dual failure: effective forgetting methods fail under compression, while quantization-robust methods fail to achieve meaningful forgetting.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective coverage of LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in STRIDE, derived from a 507-leaf taxonomy.
This methodology allows for benchmark-external validation, assessing the overall threat surface coverage of existing security studies.

stories.title: SANA-WM, a 2.6B open-source world model for 1-minute 720p video
stories.author: mjgil
bullet_points: Introduction of SANA-WM, a new open-source world model.
The model is capable of generating 1-minute, 720p video.
Its size is substantial at 2.6 billion parameters, indicating significant computational scope.

stories.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
stories.author: gemini-enterprise
bullet_points: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available.
It is being deployed on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.
The model is highlighted for being fast and cost-efficient.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline being showcased is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern deep learning ecosystem.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging apps to local AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using local or cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is the zero-configuration setup, eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within Ollama.
It facilitates the use of advanced proprietary models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settle suit over harm to students
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok have settled a lawsuit concerning the impact of social media on students.
The suit, filed by a Kentucky school district, alleged that social media addiction has caused learning disruption and a mental health crisis.
The legal action claims these issues have resulted in significant financial strain on public school budgets.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The article discusses the increased surveillance and interest in Geostationary Orbit (GEO) by major global powers (US, China, Russia).
It highlights a technical vulnerability: many satellites are not sufficiently stealthy and are visible against the blackness of space.
The content points to a growing geopolitical competition in the space domain, moving beyond traditional silent operations.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for evaluating long-range, multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge in video generation: maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) across long sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, offering a more robust evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper discusses two approaches to visual reasoning: agentic (using code/tools) and latent (using hidden embeddings).
Direct image generation during reasoning is computationally expensive and architecturally complex.
Agentic methods suffer from latency due to external execution context switching.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying real-world events chronologically, allowing agents to interact with historical data as it unfolded.
This setup enables agents to forecast future events beyond their knowledge cutoff while maintaining chronological context.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a new method for native 3D scene editing using a feed-forward architecture.
It addresses the limitation of current 3D reconstruction models, which struggle with dynamic, interactive human instructions.
The technique moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies for scene modification.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D geometric consistency of generative video models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for this evaluation.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders to provide diagnostic metrics for geometric failures.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in training large, unified Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and optimization instability when co-training diverse physical simulations (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media).
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible demands from different PDE regimes.

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: OpenDeepThink addresses the challenge of improving LLM reasoning by scaling compute breadth rather than just depth.
It tackles the 'selection bottleneck' inherent in parallel reasoning by avoiding noisy, pointwise LLM judging.
The framework utilizes pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation to select the best reasoning candidate from a population.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes using a Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment approach to combine unstructured text (narratives) and structured EHR data.
The goal is to improve patient trajectory modeling and risk forecasting in complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper critiques current AI governance frameworks for demanding safety claims that are beyond the verifiable scope of existing assurance methods.
Key safety properties required by governance (e.g., absence of hidden objectives, bounded catastrophic capability) cannot be fully verified by current behavioral evaluations or red-teaming.
The core issue is the epistemological limitation of current assurance methodologies when faced with complex AI safety requirements.

stories.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in latent diffusion models where decoders are often unconditional despite heavily conditioned denoising networks.
Proposes RefDecoder, an architectural enhancement that applies equal conditioning to the decoder to improve visual generation.
Aims to resolve issues of detail loss and inconsistency in video generation by preserving structural integrity.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test adaptive AI agents.
FutureSim achieves this by replaying real-world events chronologically, mimicking dynamic environments.
The system allows agents to forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with a historical timeline.

stories.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
stories.author: Thomas Dooms
bullet_points: Introduces 'tensor similarity,' a new weight-based metric for comparing components of neural networks.
Addresses limitations of existing similarity measures which either rely on empirical behavior or are sensitive to parameter choices.
Aims to advance mechanistic interpretability by providing a robust way to verify if two network parts implement the same computation, independent of weight-space symmetries.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in training large, unified Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
The problem arises from co-training disparate physics regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media) which cause gradient conflicts in dense models.
Proposes a solution using a Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing mechanism to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening in medical imaging.
Addresses limitations in current models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to improve screening performance and transparency.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes a multimodal approach combining unstructured clinical narratives (for context) and structured EHR data (for temporal precision).
The method utilizes Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to improve patient trajectory modeling, particularly for complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper critiques current AI governance frameworks for demanding safety verifications that are epistemically impossible for existing methods to guarantee.
Key safety claims required by governance (e.g., absence of hidden objectives, bounded capability) exceed the scope of current behavioral assurance techniques.
Current assurance methodologies, such as behavioral evaluations and red-teaming, are limited to observable model outputs, creating a verification gap.

stories.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
stories.author: Ruoshi Wen
bullet_points: The research addresses compounding errors in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models during complex, dexterous robotic manipulation.
It tackles the challenge of applying Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) to high-DoF robotic hands.
A key hurdle identified is the 'command mismatch' during human takeover, leading to abrupt policy execution errors.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework designed to update Large Language Models (LLMs) without retraining the core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, which hinders timely, domain-specific knowledge integration.
MeMo encodes new knowledge into a dedicated memory model, offering an efficient mechanism for knowledge injection.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It proposes On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability when transferring OPSD to complex, multi-turn agent interactions.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The system achieves this by replaying real-world events chronologically, allowing agents to interact with historical data streams (like news articles).
FutureSim enables agents to forecast future events beyond their training knowledge cutoff while maintaining chronological context.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper addresses the gap in systematically comparing retrieval strategy choices within LLM agentic search systems.
It focuses on the interaction between retrieval methods, agent architecture, and tool-calling paradigms.
The research is relevant to the growing adoption of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in complex, autonomous agent workflows.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment for this timeline reconstruction, relevant to complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses a gap where existing evaluations often neglect the preservation of fine-grained visual evidence in long-term memory.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to reason over changing visual states, moving beyond simple textual traces.

stories.title: Δ-Mem: Efficient Online Memory for Large Language Models
stories.author: 44za12
bullet_points: Introduces 'Δ-Mem,' a new system designed for efficient online memory management.
The technology is specifically targeted at addressing memory constraints in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Suggests an advancement in making LLMs more practical for real-world, large-scale applications.

stories.title: I Added Three Rules to Gemma 4. The MoE Searched. The Dense Model Refused.
stories.author: alimafana
bullet_points: The article compares the performance of Gemma 4 (MoE vs. Dense) against GPT-4o models in a practical Arabic e-commerce chatbot scenario.
A key finding suggests architectural differences (MoE vs. Dense) lead to divergent behavior when specific prompt rules are applied.
The MoE variant successfully navigated a catalog, while the dense variant failed under the same conditions, highlighting architectural sensitivity.

stories.title: I Built an Agent That Actually Reviews Your Pull Requests
stories.author: aditya_rasal
bullet_points: The article details the creation of an AI agent specifically designed for pull request reviews.
It addresses the existing challenges and limitations within current AI-driven code review processes.
The project is submitted as part of the Hermes Agent Challenge, indicating a focus on practical AI application.

stories.title: Mapping the Shadows: RecomendeMe Intelligence and the Revolution in Combating Human Trafficking in Brazil
stories.author: sampseiol1
bullet_points: The article discusses the application of AI and Knowledge Graphs by RecomendeMe Intelligence.
The core focus is on using advanced technology to combat the serious issue of human trafficking in Brazil.
It represents a case study in applying specialized AI solutions to complex social problems.

stories.title: Hardening Algolia MCP Server the same way I hardened Notion MCP: seven small filters
stories.author: mukundakatta
bullet_points: The article discusses securing Algolia's MCP server, which grants AI models (like Claude/Cursor) live, bidirectional access to search indices.
The proposed security pattern involves wrapping the core server with seven small utility MCPs for enhanced control.
These utility layers are designed to scan content, redact PII, manage spending, and verify write operations before production updates.
